message:
fix(streaming): idle-timeout reset on chunk, SSE oversized event boundary, provider hardening

Refactor HTTP response body streaming to use a shared
wrap_stream_with_idle_timeout helper that resets the idle timer on each
received chunk instead of measuring elapsed time from stream start. This
prevents long-running but actively-streaming responses from being killed
by the timeout. The text() method now delegates to the same stream
wrapper, eliminating duplicate timeout logic.

Fix the SSE parser to emit an event boundary (with empty data) when a
single data line exceeds the per-event byte cap. Previously the event
was silently dropped, which could cause downstream state machines to hang
waiting for a completion event that never arrives.

Set finished=true in the Cohere SSE provider when process_event returns
an error, preventing infinite retry loops on malformed events.

Replace Iterator::position with memchr::memchr for newline scanning in
the OpenAI Responses NDJSON stream parser — a focused performance
improvement for high-throughput streaming.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
